next_steps: Interrogate the ICD to evaluate for underlying rhythm and device therapy history
Check serum digoxin level, potassium, magnesium, and creatinine
Obtain cardiac troponin, electrolytes, and repeat NT-proBNP
Perform bedside transthoracic echocardiogram to assess LV function and prosthetic valve status
Initiate fluid resuscitation with caution given the history of heart failure
Prepare for emergent electrical cardioversion if the patient becomes hemodynamically unstable
Immediate 12-lead ECG to distinguish SVT from VT
ICD interrogation to review stored rhythm episodes and device logs
Stat laboratory evaluation including cardiac troponin, electrolytes (K, Mg), TSH, free T4, and repeat NT-proBNP
Bedside focused cardiac ultrasound (POCUS) to assess LV function and prosthetic valve motion
Cautious management of blood pressure and heart rate, avoiding empiric AV-nodal blockade due to hypotension and potential for VT
Review of medication adherence and recent INR check
